reason_for_decision: The article meets the pass bar with a clean search-versus-confirmation framing, strong handoff emphasis, and good practical deployment guidance.
strengths: The article avoids a false one-sensor winner and instead assigns radar and cameras different operational jobs.
Its discussion of cueing and sensor handoff is more useful than a generic comparison table alone.
The writeup stays concrete about field-of-view, pointing, and timing limits that affect real camera performance.
summary_assessment: This version passes comfortably. It is organized well, aligned with security workflows, and especially strong on explaining why radar-to-camera handoff quality matters.
